data.title: Powell hints Fed is shifting policy focus from inflation to employment
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell emphasized that given signs that the labor market is "really cooling," the Fed is leaning toward achieving "maximum employment" in its dual mandate after a long period of increased focus on inflation. He said the risk of persistently high inflation has decreased since April, partly due to slower job growth. The Fed's baseline assumption is that the impact of tariffs on inflation is likely to be short-lived. On the other hand, he said, downside risks to the labor market have increased, as job creation appears to be below the so-called "break-even rate" needed to keep the unemployment rate unchanged. (Jinshi)

data.title: Brera Holdings Rebrands as Solmate, Raises $300 Million for SOL Treasury
data.description:
Solmate, formerly Brera Holdings, emerges with $300 million for a Solana-based treasury, led by ex-Kraken exec Marco Santori.

data.title: U.S. stock indexes extended losses, with the Nasdaq down 1%.
data.description: During Powell's speech, U.S. stock indexes fell further, with the Nasdaq down 1%, the S&P 500 down 0.6%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.05%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed Chairman Powell: Today's rate cut can be seen as a risk-mitigating rate cut
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell: There is no broad support for a 50 basis point rate cut today. Today's rate cut can be seen as a risk mitigation cut. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed Chairman Powell: Core PCE rose 2.9% in August compared with the same period last year
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell: The overall consumer price index (PCE) in August is likely to rise by 2.7% compared with the same period last year, and the core PCE will rise by 2.9% compared with the same period last year. (Jinshi)

data.title: XRP Price Prediction: Analyst Sees $6 Target by November
data.description:
Analyst EGRAG Crypto argues that XRP could push toward $6–$7 before mid-November if current momentum holds.
source: https://coindoo.com/market/xrp-price-prediction-analyst-sees-6-target-by-november/

data.title: Fed officials expect no more rate cuts next year
data.description: Nick Timiraos, a Fed spokesperson, said the Fed's forecasts highlight that future decisions may be more controversial: 7 of the 19 participants expected no further rate cuts this year, while two expected only one more rate cut. These data suggest that most officials do not expect more rate cuts next year given the current expectations of strong economic activity. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed dot plot: overall more accommodative, one official expects a 150 basis point rate cut this year
data.description: The Fed's dot plot shows that among the 19 officials, one believes there will be no rate cuts in 2025 (compared to 7 officials in June), six believe there should be a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut in 2025, or one rate cut (compared to 2 officials in June), two believe there should be a cumulative 50 basis point rate cut in 2025, or two rate cuts (compared to 8 officials in June), and nine believe there should be a cumulative 75 basis point rate cut in 2025, or three rate cuts (compared to 2 officials in June). One official (most likely Milan) believes there should be a substantial 150 basis point rate cut in 2025, indicating at least two more substantial rate cuts (75 basis points + 50 basis points) this year. (Jinshi)

data.title: The probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates in October has risen to 94%.
data.description: Following today's FOMC statement, U.S. interest rate futures are pricing in a 94% chance of a Fed rate cut in October, compared to a 71.6% probability before the statement.

data.title: Spot gold hits new high on Fed decision day
data.description: After the Fed’s decision was announced, spot gold hit a record high of $3,704 per barrel, breaking the previous high of $3,703.1 per ounce, and has risen by more than 40% this year (Gold Ten)

data.description: The US Federal Reserve's interest rate decision (upper limit) for September 17th is 4.25%, in line with expectations and the previous reading of 4.50%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed forecasts: 9 of 19 officials expect two more rate cuts in 2025
data.description: The Fed's forecasts show that nine of 19 officials expect two more rate cuts in 2025, two expect one, and six expect no further rate cuts.

data.title: Two Fed officials expect two rate cuts this year
data.description: The Fed's dot plot shows that among the 19 officials, two believe that interest rates will be cut by 50 basis points in total by 2025, or two times (compared to eight in June).
